message:
test(api): add explicit assertions for recipient deduplication

The "deduplicates recipients" test case had comments explaining expected
behavior but lacked actual assertions to verify it. This could allow
regressions to slip through unnoticed.

Added explicit assertions that verify:

1. Message recipients are properly deduplicated:
   - Input: ["Claude", "Claude", "Codex", "claude"]
   - Expected: ["Claude", "Codex", "claude"] (3 unique, case-sensitive)
   - Verifies duplicate "Claude" is removed while "claude" (different case)
     is preserved as a separate recipient

2. Deliveries are created for each unique recipient:
   - Confirms 3 deliveries exist (one per unique recipient)
   - Validates each expected recipient name appears in deliveries

This strengthens the test from "documents behavior in comments" to
"actively verifies behavior with assertions", providing regression
protection for the recipient deduplication logic.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
